text: An unnamed law clerk used Lexis+ AI and LexisNexis Protégé to research a filing, generating at least eight false case citation hallucinations. Supervising attorney Anderson signed the brief without verifying the AI output. The court found both co-counsel violated Rule 11 but imposed the $4,000 monetary sanction only on Anderson, crediting the firm's sworn account of the law clerk's AI tool use without rejecting the LexisNexis attribution.
ai_system_name: Lexis+ AI and LexisNexis Protégé (named; court-found via firm's sworn account)
deployer: Attorney Anderson (Anderson and Associates, specially admitted NY co-counsel)
canonical_source_conflicted: Eight non-existent federal case citations; Fed. R. Civ. P. 11
mitigation_gap: Supervising attorney failed to verify AI-generated research before signing and filing; AI detectors flagged the case explanations as 100% AI-generated.
reliance_or_harm: $4,000 Rule 11 sanction on supervising attorney; local co-counsel found to have violated Rule 11 but not fined.
